definition:
1. a bean , Lat. faba, (Iliad by Homer) 
2. the lot  by which public officers were elected at Athens (because those who drew white beans  were chosen), ὁ τῷ κυάμῳ λαχών an officer chosen by lot , (Herdotus Historicus); βουλὴ ἡ ἀπὸ τοῦ κυάμου (Thucydides); ἄρχοντας ἀπὸ κυάμου καθιστάναι (Xenophon Historicus) (ML)
